rationale: A precision strike will exploit our operational window and shatter enemy morale while avoiding escalation to full warfare.
Forward-deploying our Black Sea fleet reinforces pressure on Tethys and secures maritime lines for future operations.
A rapid, shallow incursion will test enemy defenses and create leverage for political gains in ceasefire negotiations.
Strengthening our critical infrastructure defenses is essential to prevent further disruptions to energy systems and maintain operational continuity amid escalating cyber threats.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will disrupt enemy espionage networks seeking to exploit our economic vulnerabilities and internal dissent.
A coordinated information campaign will reinforce domestic and international perceptions of resolve and legitimacy, offsetting Western narrative dominance.

Targeting key Tethysian financial institutions and defense-sector supply chains will amplify economic pressure without triggering broad humanitarian fallout that could erode our international neutrality bloc support.
Seizing offshore assets linked to Tethysian leadership exploits our intelligence leads and signals resolve, complementing military strikes by increasing the cost of continued resistance.
Controlled devaluation of our currency will bolster export competitiveness and offset sanction-driven import shortfalls, stabilizing domestic markets amid escalating financial warfare.
A visible demonstration of combat readiness will exploit their command restructuring and signal resolve without triggering escalation.
Large-scale drills will accelerate force integration and signal long-term commitment to domestic and allied audiences.
Increasing frontline vigilance will deter opportunistic advances during this fragile stabilization phase.
With Novaris restructuring commands and recent cyber attacks on critical infrastructure, hardening our own systems is essential to prevent exploitation during this fragile stabilization.
Given signs of prolonged conflict preparation, we must disrupt their intelligence networks to protect operational security of our forces and allies.
A coordinated narrative emphasizing our resilience and their instability will reinforce international support without provoking escalation.
Given moderate existing sanctions and strong international support, we can precisely target key sectors financing Novaris’s military without triggering broader economic fallout.
Seizing assets of sanctioned entities undermines their war economy and signals resolve, complementing military and cyber efforts without escalating kinetic risks.
Engaging neutral markets now will diversify our economic partnerships and reduce long-term vulnerability to supply shocks amid ongoing sanctions pressure.
With international support at 80%, we must institutionalize alliances through formal agreements that lock in military, economic, and diplomatic coordination to sustain pressure on Novaris.
Hosting a multilateral summit under regional auspices will reinforce liberal institutional norms and demonstrate that our cause is collectively owned, not unilateral.
Deploying observer missions to conflict zones bolsters transparency, deters atrocities, and preserves our moral high ground amid ongoing stabilization efforts.
